channel.title: Features on CrySPY
channel.description: Recent content in Features on CrySPY

item.title: Backup
item.description: CrySPY has a simple backup function. The following files are backed up: cryspy.in cryspy.stat log_cryspy err_cryspy calc_in/* data/* ext/* work/* are NOT included. Note that only one generation is backed up, and any earlier backups will be deleted. Auto backup The timing of the automatic backup is as follows: before going to next selection (BO, LAQA) or next generation (EA) append structures Manual backup To manually back up, run cryspy with -b or --backup option as:

item.title: Clean
item.description: CrySPY has a simple clean (just move files) function. It is useful when you want to start over from the beginning. The following files are cleaned up: cryspy.stat log_cryspy err_cryspy lock_cryspy data/* work/* ext/* tmp_calc_FP/* tmp_gen_struc/* To clean up, run cryspy with -c or --clean option as: $ ls calc_in cryspy.in cryspy.stat data err_cryspy log_cryspy $ cryspy -c Are you sure you want to clean the data? 'yes' or 'no' [y/n]: y $ ls calc_in cryspy.

item.title: CrySPY_ID in job files
item.description: In the job file of CrySPY, the string “CrySPY_ID” is automatically replaced with the structure ID. When you use a job scheduler such as PBS and SLURM, it is useful to set the structure ID to the job name. For example, in the PBS system, #PBS -N Si_CrySPY_ID in ID 10 is replaced with #PBS -N Si_10. Note that starting with a number will result in an error. You should add a prefix like Si_.
